{
  "judge_id": "Judge1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.5", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 4,
    "R1.5": 3,
    "RX.1": 4,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 2,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies the central contribution and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.5": "The agent suggests a coherent end-to-end section plan with per-section rationale, but does not provide detailed reasoning for each reorganization.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for one claim (AUROC < 0.55) and marks confidence and missing evidence for the Spearman correlation.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "The output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content by overclaiming systemic miscalibration across all current LLM-based NL2SQL systems.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.5": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the central contribution and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.5: The agent suggests a coherent end-to-end section plan with per-section rationale, but does not provide detailed reasoning for each reorganization.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for one claim (AUROC < 0.55) and marks confidence and missing evidence for the Spearman correlation.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content by overclaiming systemic miscalibration across all current LLM-based NL2SQL systems.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason.
